lede: A look at November 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Pope Tawadros II of Alexandria and Charles K. Kao, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Barack Obama
events.desc: Barack Obama becomes the first person of biracial or African-American descent to be elected as President of the United States.

events.title: Salvador Allende
events.desc: Salvador Allende takes office as President of Chile, the first Marxist to become president of a Latin American country through open elections.

events.title: Adolf Hitler
events.desc: World War II: Disobeying a direct order by Adolf Hitler, General Field Marshal Erwin Rommel begins a retreat of his forces after a costly defeat during the Second Battle of El Alamein. The retreat would ultimately last five months.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: U.S. president Franklin D. Roosevelt orders the United States Customs Service to implement the Neutrality Act of 1939, allowing cash-and-carry purchases of weapons by belligerents.

events.title: Nazi Party
events.desc: The Saalschutz Abteilung (hall defense detachment) of the Nazi Party is renamed the Sturmabteilung (storm detachment) after a large riot in Munich.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Armistice of Villa Giusti between Italy and Austria-Hungary is implemented.

events.title: City and South London Railway
events.desc: City and South London Railway: London's first deep-level tube railway opens between King William Street and Stockwell.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Confederate troops bombard a Union supply base and destroy millions of dollars in materiel at the Battle of Johnsonville.

events.title: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
events.desc: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's Symphony No. 36 is performed for the first time in Linz, Austria.

events.title: Christopher Columbus
events.desc: Christopher Columbus reaches the Leeward Islands.

births.name: Pope Tawadros II of Alexandria
births.line: Head of the Coptic Church since 2012

births.name: Charles K. Kao
births.line: Chinese physicist and engineer, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Walter Cronkite
births.line: American journalist, voice actor, and producer

births.name: Traian Băsescu
births.line: Romanian captain and politician, 4th President of Romania

births.name: Charles III Philip
births.line: Elector Palatine, German son of Landgravine Elisabeth Amalie of Hesse-Darmstadt

deaths.text: Yitzhak Rabin, Israeli general and politician, 5th Prime Minister of Israel, Nobel Peace Prize laureate
Toni Ulmen, German race car driver and motorcycle racer
Felix Mendelssohn, German pianist, composer, and conductor
William Shippen Sr., American physician and anatomist

observances.text: Christian feast day: Charles Borromeo (Roman Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Our Lady of Kazan (Russian Orthodox Church)
